2604.14849.title: Efficient Search of Implantable Adaptive Cells for Medical Image Segmentation
2604.14849.paper_summary: Purpose: Adaptive skip modules can improve medical image segmentation, but searching for them is computationally costly. Implantable Adaptive Cells (IACs) are compact NAS modules inserted into U-Net skip connections, reducing the search space compared with full-network NAS. However, the original IAC framework still requires a 200-epoch differentiable search for each backbone and dataset. Methods: We analyzed the temporal behavior of operations and edges within IAC cells during differentiable search on public medical image segmentation benchmarks. We found that operations selected in the final discrete cell typically emerge among the strongest candidates early in training, and their architecture parameters stabilize well before the final epoch. Based on this, we propose a Jensen--Shannon-divergence-based stability criterion that tracks per-edge operation-importance distributions and progressively prunes low-importance operations during search. The accelerated framework is called IAC-LTH. Results: Across four public benchmarks (ACDC, BraTS, KiTS, AMOS), several 2-D U-Net backbones, and a 2-D nnU-Net pipeline, IAC-LTH discovers IAC cells whose patient-level segmentation performance matches and sometimes slightly exceeds that of cells found by the original full-length search, while reducing wall-clock NAS cost by 3.7x to 16x across datasets and backbones. These results are consistent across architectures, benchmarks, and both non-augmented and augmented training settings, while preserving the gains of IAC-equipped U-Nets over strong attention-based and dense-skip baselines. Conclusion: Competitive IAC architectures can be identified from early-stabilizing operations without running the full search, making adaptive skip-module design more practical for medical image segmentation under realistic computational constraints.
2604.14849.paper_summary_zh: 目的：自適應跳躍模塊可以改善醫學影像分割，但搜尋它們的計算成本高昂。可植入自適應細胞（IACs）是插入到 U-Net 跳躍連接中的緊湊 NAS 模塊，與全網絡 NAS 相比，減少了搜尋空間。然而，原始的 IAC 框架仍然需要對每個骨幹和數據集進行 200 代的可微分搜尋。方法：我們分析了在公共醫學影像分割基準上進行可微分搜尋時 IAC 細胞內操作和邊緣的時間行為。我們發現，最終離散細胞中選擇的操作通常在訓練早期就出現於最強候選者之中，並且它們的架構參數在最終代之前就已穩定。基於此，我們提出了一種基於詹森-香農散度的穩定性標準，該標準跟踪每個邊緣的操作重要性分佈，並在搜尋過程中逐步修剪低重要性的操作。加速框架稱為 IAC-LTH。結果：在四個公共基準（ACDC、BraTS、KiTS、AMOS）、幾個 2-D U-Net 骨幹和一個 2-D nnU-Net 流水線上，IAC-LTH 發現的 IAC 細胞在患者級別的分割性能與原始全長搜尋找到的細胞相匹配，並且有時略微超過，同時在各數據集和骨幹上將牆鐘 NAS 成本降低了 3.7 倍至 16 倍。這些結果在架構、基準以及非增強和增強訓練設置中都是一致的，同時保留了 IAC 裝備的 U-Net 在強注意力基準和密集跳躍基準上的優勢。結論：可以從早期穩定的操作中識別出具有競爭力的 IAC 架構，而無需進行完整搜尋，這使得在現實計算限制下，自適應跳躍模塊設計對於醫學影像分割變得更為實用。
2604.14849.author: Emil Benedykciuk et.al.
2604.14849.authors: Emil Benedykciuk, Marcin Denkowski, Grzegorz M. Wójcik
